pattern: Values are tiny (<=50), so keep one ancestor stack per possible value and, at each node, pick the deepest ancestor whose value is coprime with the current node.
hints: Node values are bounded by 50, so there are only a handful of distinct values to reason about.
During a DFS from the root, remember for every value the most recent (deepest) ancestor that carried it.
At each node, among the values coprime with it, choose the ancestor sitting at the greatest depth; track depths to break ties toward the closest ancestor.

edge_inputs.description: root value 1 is coprime with everything below
chain where no pair is coprime

stress.description: random tree by attaching each new node to an earlier one
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 51) for _ in range(n)]
    edges = []
    for v in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randrange(0, v)
        edges.append([u, v])
    return [nums, edges]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def getCoprimes(self, nums, edges):
        from math import gcd
        n = len(nums)
        adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for u, v in edges:
            adj[u].append(v)
            adj[v].append(u)
        parent = [-1] * n
        depth = [0] * n
        order = []
        visited = [False] * n
        stack = [0]
        visited[0] = True
        while stack:
            node = stack.pop()
            order.append(node)
            for nb in adj[node]:
                if not visited[nb]:
                    visited[nb] = True
                    parent[nb] = node
                    depth[nb] = depth[node] + 1
                    stack.append(nb)
        ans = [-1] * n
        for node in order:
            cur = parent[node]
            best = -1
            bestdepth = -1
            while cur != -1:
                if gcd(nums[node], nums[cur]) == 1 and depth[cur] > bestdepth:
                    best = cur
                    bestdepth = depth[cur]
                    break
                cur = parent[cur]
            ans[node] = best
        return ans
